item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The image "map-final" in the Sampi-3 Logs still contains an "SCP-5000".

I think the ending of this scip is disappointing. It's very bottom heavy: so much exposition and several characters introduced in the last selection of retrieved documents, with little to no hinting at this stuff previously. The final twist in the offset is also kind of cheesy. I think it's a real shame because the ending being a bit of a let down means it's the freshest thing in my mind as I come to writing this comment.

However, this is definitely still a +1 from me. A lot of the mysteries submitted for 5000 have bored me or put me off reading right away, but this one I all went through in one setting. Even though I don't really like the great big revelation at the end, the backstory and contents of that revelation are still great. You present a gripping mystery and this is really helped by the brilliant use of media. I love how retro this all is: the spectrographs, the fuzzy videos, the robot voice, the black and green computer docs, the faded papers pulled out of archives. Like you said in your own comment, definite vibes of old series Doctor Who.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I upvoted the first 50% or so of the article, but when we got to the part of the actual origin of the tower, and how the older 05 council basically overrode the Ethic Committee (With the why and how they did it unexplained), I basically lost all engagement. Something about the war with the Soviets and GRU-P, but there's so little information given that I cant fathom a plausible motivation for it. The ending itself was a bit of an eye roll as well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've been putting this off for awhile. Great stuff, Jackal. The very last addendum was a little over the top, but it succeeded in catching me off-guard.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: missed a couple “scp-5000”s in there, 2 in the sampi 3 logs- one image, one image subtitle

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The dates were confusing for me too, but I figured that all the matrix stuff went down in the 80s, because it states the aesthetics are a mix of the 60s to 80s, and the town wasnt subject to outside media.

item.title: No vote from me
item.content:encoded:
At first, I read this through and upvoted it. Then I kept scrolling, clicked the last bonus addendum, read it, then retracted my vote.

It is rare that I believe that the best alternative for something in an article is nothing as there's usually some better idea I can give, but in this case there isn't. The foundation systems being hijacked by a megalomaniacal AI is less interesting of an ending than the 05 council having to take a credibility hit for some dumb shit they did.

item.title: +1'ed
item.content:encoded:
Definitely +1 here. Yet, I have some things that I truly love and others that I would have prefered to be different, but that didn't affect my appreciation for it THAT much.

I don't even feel the need to write down them, apart from one in particular: I am not a big fan of stories that put the O5 in a too human perspective.
I know, not everyone like the idea of having O5 being a supernatural group of some kind, but O5 being brats going so directly against the EC without discussing the issue further and not doing shit about it until they are exposed?

This is the weakest point of the entry IMO. They could have done anything about it. Create false reports trough some loyal researcher or whatever and remove the AI without even giving much thought about it.
Yes, the SCP protects and doesn't nuke anomalies that aren't too dangerous, but this isn't a "real anomaly", this is a big fuck up they produced and that could be used against them at any time.
It's absolutely against any interpretation that doesn't make the O5 idiots that this thing has been out there so long without them doing anything.
What if the GOC entered it? That would have been a disaster.

So yeah, TL;DR I really like the idea and in particular the execution but I really needed to speak against this O5.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Great story, but the ending is too hammy for my tastes. Also, why do they do the first contact expedition in 2019 when the anomaly was discovered over 30 years earlier in 1987?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
all of the entries in the 5kcon are very good articles, but this, seven, and why? are probably my top three contenders for articles that FEEL like they should be the 5000 slot. phenomenal writing, the spectrogram gif freaked me way out, i love the way you made the characters feel like real people while making them distinctly inhuman.
+1 and godspeed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'll have to second Mod's critique here because I am simply too goddamn stunned at this absolute masterpiece (also, tired) to write anything nearly as meaningful.

I'll just thank you for a truly rewarding reading experience for now and call it a day. I genuinely did not think I'd come across anything in this contest that would blow me away; this did exactly that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The offset is maybe a little narmy, and this needs another pass for a handful of typos, but I think it's pretty good. Good way to explore the mystery.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I hate this dumbass tower. He's so mean. Great story though. 1+

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed, thanks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Novote because I was hyperfocused on the fact that "Domäne" is used once at the start of the article and every other time, it's "Domane".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
two enthusiastic thumbs up. the influence of doctor who can be felt all over this article. everything came together at the end, and that last page was magnificently executed. this truly felt 5000-worthy as a cinematic epic about the foundation's past failures.

+1 and godspeed in the contest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Absolutely fantastic work! I am all about cults and towers and this mixed them both with something truly unexpected. Super rad, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can honestly say that this is my favorite entry. It took me on such a ride, and the final twist is glorious.

Genuinely blown away by this entry. Like those above, the images really cinch this for me… but the slow but methodical revelations are phenomenally Executed. For my money, this is one of the few real "mysteries" of this contest…. how this can be so criminally underrated?

Above all else, the characters are fleshed out and believable, and the uneasy tension of the piece continues to grow until I was overwhelmingly of the opinion everyone should get the hell outta that town.

+1 enthusiastically.

PS: the title is straight fire.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this. There's not a lot that I can say without echoing some of the earlier comments. The feeling of uncanny is perfectly executed throughout this piece, with some parts of it eliciting that sort of "We need to get out, NOW" feeling from me. I personally liked the final addendum, as it's sort of closer to the horror movie style this piece seems to be going for. A very clear +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's pretty understandable - the last addenda is pretty hit or miss. It's also more intended to be "rogue aic" rather than completely taking over the Foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah that last addenda just sends the whole thing into narm territory, some random crazy ai from the 1960s running its own fascist town via brainwashing is perfectly ok in the foundationverse, same ai abruptly taking over the foundation via uploaded documents?
Not so much.
Null vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's a lot to like about this; the PENTAGRAM is used exceedingly well and the logs from Sampi-3 and Sampi-4 contribute to the paranoid science-fiction thriller theme. Kontakteen and the Viceroy are also fantastic characters, and the use of mixed media is aggressively good. However, I dislike the ending and think that the final spectrogram is a much better note to end on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this entry has the absolute best use of media in any entry. its very atmospheric and though it does follow typical story beats of a weird town with cursed media, it executes these tropes well and does enough to keep things fresh. my only complaints are that sometimes the media takes a while to load; maybe shrinking filesizes would be for the better. anyway, easy +1. i hope to see this climb.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, I don't think I would have without the support of my friends! I thank you for supporting me when I was feeling doubtful about my 5k entry!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeouch, this is some unbelievably high praise coming from you, Mod! I'm just appreciative that you like it so much. Really, those are some extremely kind words from you.

All I have to say is: mega thank you for leaving such a positive review!!!!!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is, in a few words, a spectacular achievement. My mind is blown, my wig has been snatched.

The amount of careful detail put into this story is so, so well done, and it excellently contributes to that terrible-unnatural-disturbance-in-a-sleepy-town trope of horror that you nail. This plays out like a damn movie, setting the atmosphere and the subsequent pacing of the investigations in a tight and gripping way.

And this is not to mention the media, which is stunning. Original images, animations, fucking videos. Some really inspired ideas like spectrograms and scanned documents and hotel notes. And what I like is that it feels intimately tied to the reading experience; the beginning image with the "LOVE THE TOWER PRAISE THE TOWER" stuff - very trope-y, but absolutely sold the eldritch creepy angle in a refreshing snappy way.

This hit uncanny valley feelings all over. The bizarre newscasts, complete with wrong-looking chilling logos - perfectly building and illustrating the feeling of takeover, the themes of propaganda and worship. The presentation - distributing details so that the situation felt gritty and real.

I consider this a masterful tribute to the horror genre - a simple story about a godlike entity playing with a small group/town (very reminiscent of Doctor Who, of course), but told so cinemetically, with such precision and scope, that it embodies the story completely, and is brought to life.

You've come so far as a writer since you started, Jackal - I'm just surprised it happened this fucking quickly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was holding off on reading this because I didn't want to be spoiled. I'm so glad I didn't.

+1, you glorious bastard. Happy you managed to finish this in time!

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
No terror shall exist in my domain: only violent understandings.

A huge undertaking authored by yours truly, JackalRelated. Made with a lot of support, especially from these fellows:

» Show
« poof!

aismallard
Modulum
Yossipossi
Rounderhouse
Sterbai - for providing the voice for Military Complex One/The Viceroy and the picture of Capis Cai!
djkaktus
Uncle Nicolini
Alces_alces


Idea inspired from a forum post by user Rainbowknishes and a helping of classic Doctor Who aestheticism. A lot of religious undertones are in this skip as well. A decent amount of it didn't pass the chopping block, but it would have dragged on had I not removed it.

Technically coldposted this since I didn't get any crit besides aesthetics prior to posting.

I worked on this for a loooong time, and I hope you all enjoy! Good luck and well wishes to the other 5kon competitors.

Writing music: Popcorn and Little Russian, both referenced in the skip.

« image links

(Sorry Japanese Branch)
